info.title: STIR/SHAKEN Certificate Management API
info.description:
The STIR/SHAKEN Certificate Management API enables telecommunications providers to manage STIR/SHAKEN certificates and profiles.
It offers secure operations for creating, updating, retrieving, and deleting certificates and profiles.

tags.name: Profiles
tags.description: See and provision Stir/Shaken Profiles at the Account level.

tags.name: Account Certificates
tags.description: See Stir/Shaken Certificates at the Account level.

tags.name: Account Certificate SPCs
tags.description: See Stir/Shaken Certificate SPCs at the Account level.

get.description: Retrieve a list of all of the stir/shaken profiles for an account.

post.description: Create a stir/shaken profile for an account.

get.description: Get a stir/shaken profile by account and stir/shaken profile id.

put.description: Update a stir/shaken profile by account and stir/shaken profile id.

delete.description: Delete an existing stir/shaken profile by account and stir/shaken profile id.

get.description: Get the Certificate used for encrypting sensitive data.
Retrieve a list of all stir/shaken certificates for an account.

post.description:
Upload a STIR/SHAKEN certificate keypair for call signing. 
Supports US and CA certificates with proper validation and security checks.

get.description: Get a stir/shaken certificate by stir/shaken certificate id.

put.description: Update a stir/shaken certificate by stir/shaken certificate id.

delete.description: Delete an existing stir/shaken certificate by account and stir/shaken certificate id.

get.description: Retrieve a list of all stir/shaken certificate SPCs for an account.

attestLevel.description: Attestation level. A (Full), B (Partial), or C (Gateway). Required for US/CAN hosted signing cert assignments. For FR hosted assignments, this can be null as the 'indirect' profileType will dictate the attestLevel.

Basic.description:
Basic authentication is a simple authentication scheme built into the HTTP protocol. To use it, send your HTTP requests with an Authorization header that contains the word Basic followed by a space and a base64-encoded string `username:password`.
Example: `Authorization: Basic ZGVtbZpwQDU1dzByZA==`

Bearer.description:
Bearer authentication (also called token authentication) is an HTTP authentication scheme that involves security tokens called bearer tokens. The name “Bearer authentication” can be understood as “give access to the bearer of this token.” The client must send this token in the Authorization header when making requests to protected resources.

Example: `Authorization: Bearer <token>`
